project_name: Firm and Institutional Decomposition for the Post-Web
simulation_requirement: Simulate what happens when firms and institutions of different types (venture capital, university, bank, media company) attempt to self-decompose into machine-readable primitives — knowledge graphs, skills, and processes — before AI agents do it without consent. Model both the internal dynamics (staff resistance, leadership conviction, knowledge encoding difficulty) and external dynamics (agent reverse-engineering speed, competitive pressure, market share shifts). Compare outcomes between firms that decompose early vs late vs refuse entirely. Include the OV case study: a venture firm going from 100 staff to 10 humans orchestrating thousands of agents.
additional_context: Based on Path 1 'Decompose' from the Post-Web thesis by Outlier Ventures, and the real-world case of OV decomposing itself over 24 months. The three institutional primitives are knowledge graphs, skills, and processes. The framework argues that self-decomposition is sovereign, external decomposition by agents is extraction, and that 'Initiator as Power User' (radical openness) is the only durable business model in the Post-Web.
